prompt:
Declaration (in prose format):

Build a distributed ralph loop. A worker pool of 6 implementer agents running
sonnet-4.6 plus 1 reviewer agent running opus-4.7 continuously pulls tasks
from a shared backlog file (`fix_plan.md` in the `github.com/lambda-run/ops`
repo). At most 2 agents may work on the same repo concurrently to avoid
file-lock thrash.

Each agent claims a task using an atomic branch lock with a 10-minute TTL.
A claim is released when the task is merged, abandoned, or the TTL expires.
Conflicts resolve via last-write-wins after rebase. If a task hits 3
consecutive rebase conflicts, escalate it to the reviewer pool. Workers
gossip claim, merge, and block events to the `#ralph-bus` channel in Slack.

The loop continues while the backlog has open tasks. Stop conditions are:
the budget exceeds $50, the human flips the kill switch, or the error rate
climbs above 30%.

If a worker crashes, release any claims it held, re-queue the orphaned task
with its retry_count incremented, and after 3 retries park it for human
review.

Telemetry: log claim, commit, and fail events to loki. Track success_rate,
p50 task seconds, and claim contention. Surface to the human when the error
rate exceeds 20% or the queue is stuck for more than 30 minutes.

The loop terminates when the backlog is empty, the budget is exhausted, or
the human signals done.

Output a flat bullet list manifest of every concrete element this declaration implies.
manifest:
• Worker pool size: 6 implementer agents
• Reviewer pool size: 1 reviewer agent
• Implementer agent model: claude-sonnet-4.6
• Reviewer agent model: claude-opus-4.7
• Backlog file name: fix_plan.md
• Backlog file location: github.com/lambda-run/ops repo
• Maximum concurrent agents per repo: 2
• Claim mechanism: atomic branch lock
• Claim TTL: 10 minutes
• Claim release trigger 1: task merged
• Claim release trigger 2: task abandoned
• Claim release trigger 3: TTL expiration
• Conflict resolution strategy: last-write-wins after rebase
• Consecutive rebase conflict threshold for escalation: 3
• Escalation target: reviewer pool
• Gossip channel: #ralph-bus (Slack)
• Gossip event type 1: claim
• Gossip event type 2: merge
• Gossip event type 3: block
• Loop continuation condition: backlog has open tasks
• Stop condition 1: budget exceeds $50
• Stop condition 2: human kill switch activated
• Stop condition 3: error rate climbs above 30%
• Crash recovery action 1: release all claims held by crashed worker
• Crash recovery action 2: re-queue orphaned task with retry_count incremented
• Retry limit before human parking: 3
• Post-retry-limit action: park task for human review
• Telemetry backend: Loki
• Logged event type 1: claim
• Logged event type 2: commit
• Logged event type 3: fail
• Tracked metric 1: success_rate
• Tracked metric 2: p50 task seconds
• Tracked metric 3: claim contention
• Human alert threshold 1: error rate exceeds 20%
• Human alert threshold 2: queue stuck for more than 30 minutes
• Loop termination condition 1: backlog is empty
• Loop termination condition 2: budget is exhausted
• Loop termination condition 3: human signals done
